scenes.title: Hook: The Workflow Promise
scenes.narration: What if a full storyboard was just a paste away? This is a walkthrough of a workflow that takes you from a raw brief to a finished, editable storyboard, in one clean pass.
scenes.visual_prompt: Slow-motion close-up of fingers releasing a keyboard shortcut on a sleek laptop in a dim editorial workspace. Warm amber desk lamp creates a narrow pool of light. The screen glows a soft blue-white as a text cursor blinks in a large input field. Camera drifts in gently from behind-left, rack-focusing on the screen. Atmosphere: focused, anticipatory, premium product-demo editorial.
scenes.staging_notes: Open on the keyboard gesture, drift toward the screen, let the cursor blink hold for a beat before the on-screen text fades in. Mood is quiet confidence.
on_screen_text: From brief to storyboard in one pass.

scenes.title: Workflow Roadmap
scenes.narration: Four steps. Paste your brief, get your storyboard, edit any scene you need to, then preview the result. That's the whole loop.
scenes.visual_prompt: Dark navy background with a clean horizontal four-step workflow diagram. Each step is a numbered pill node connected by thin arrows. Steps read: Paste Brief, Get Storyboard, Edit Scene, Preview Result. Minimal sans-serif typography, nodes light up left-to-right in sequence. Premium editorial finish.
scenes.staging_notes: Reveal each step node sequentially with a subtle pop-in and connecting line draw. Hold the full diagram for a beat at the end before transitioning. Keep it sparse and fast.

scenes.title: The Empty Workspace
scenes.narration: The workspace opens clean. A brief input panel on the left, an empty storyboard panel on the right. Nothing between you and the idea.
scenes.visual_prompt: Wide editorial screenshot-style illustration of a split-panel software interface on a dark charcoal background. Left panel shows a large empty text input field with a placeholder label and a subtle cursor. Right panel is empty with a light grid pattern suggesting awaiting content. Clean sans-serif UI chrome, thin dividers, soft panel shadow depth. Premium SaaS aesthetic, cool-dark palette, subtle ambient glow from the screen edges. No readable text inside the panels beyond placeholder UI labels.
scenes.staging_notes: Overlay labels appear as soft callout tags pointing to each panel. Feel clean and minimal.

scenes.title: Pasting the Brief
scenes.narration: The user pastes in the brief. A paragraph, a few requirements, a tone note. Whatever you have.
scenes.visual_prompt: Tight cinematic close-up of a software interface in a dark-mode workspace. A text block appears in a left-panel input field as if just pasted — text populates from top to bottom with a subtle blink. Camera is slightly angled, shallow depth of field, monitor edges slightly blurred. Muted blue-charcoal UI with warm ambient light from off-screen. Action feels deliberate and unhurried. No lip-sync talker.
scenes.staging_notes: The key visual beat is the text appearing in the input field — make the paste action feel satisfying and purposeful. Short clip, 3-4 seconds.

scenes.title: One Click to Generate
scenes.narration: One click. The tool processes the brief and begins building the storyboard automatically.
scenes.visual_prompt: Dark charcoal editorial interface illustration showing a left panel with filled text and a prominent action button at the bottom. The button is highlighted with a subtle glow ring suggesting it is being activated. A thin horizontal progress bar below the button begins to fill from left to right. Right panel still empty but now shows a faint skeleton loader pattern. Cool-dark palette, crisp UI chrome, soft inner glow. No readable body text, only UI affordance labels as overlay targets.
scenes.staging_notes: Deterministic overlay places the button label precisely. Subtle animation hint on the progress bar feel.

scenes.title: Storyboard Appears
scenes.narration: The storyboard populates. Scene cards appear in sequence, each one carrying a title, narration, and visual prompt.
scenes.visual_prompt: Cinematic overhead tilt-down onto a software interface on a large monitor in a dark editorial workspace. The right storyboard panel fills with a cascade of scene cards appearing top to bottom, each card containing structured text fields. Camera slowly pushes in toward the screen from above. Warm rim light on the monitor edge. The atmosphere is a satisfying reveal — precise, ordered, and immediate. No lip-sync talker.
scenes.staging_notes: The cascade of scene cards is the hero moment here. Camera push-in should feel anticipatory. Cards should be clearly visible as structured items, not blurs.

scenes.title: Scene Card Anatomy
scenes.narration: Each scene card has the same anatomy: a title, spoken narration, a visual prompt, and optional fields for overlays or staging notes.
scenes.visual_prompt: Single expanded scene card on a dark navy background. Card is centered with clear typographic zones. Labeled regions are highlighted in sequence with soft colored callout lines. Card areas: Title at top, Narration below, Visual Prompt below that, then a compact row of optional fields at the bottom. Clean sans-serif, premium product UI editorial.
scenes.staging_notes: Reveal each labeled region one by one with a callout highlight animation — a soft underline or side bar appears in sequence. Pacing should match narration beats. End with all four labeled simultaneously.

scenes.title: Clicking Into a Scene
scenes.narration: Click any card to open it. The editor loads that scene's fields inline, ready to change.
scenes.visual_prompt: Dark charcoal editorial software interface illustration showing the storyboard panel with one scene card visually selected — a subtle highlight border and elevated shadow around it. To the right, an inline edit panel slides into view showing individual fields: Title, Narration, Visual Prompt as editable text inputs. Clean vertical layout, thin field separators, small field labels above each input. Soft ambient glow from screen edges, premium SaaS aesthetic. No readable body text in the fields, only structural UI shape.
scenes.staging_notes: Overlay label appears as a soft tooltip near the selected card. Inline edit panel slides in from right.

scenes.title: Making the Edit
scenes.narration: The user updates the narration. A few words replaced, a detail sharpened. Then save.
scenes.visual_prompt: Tight cinematic close-up of hands typing on a keyboard while a dark-mode software editor is visible on screen behind them. The text field in focus shows a cursor mid-sentence, text being selected and retyped. Monitor glow illuminates the hands from below. Shallow depth of field, monitor slightly blurred but narration field clearly active. Camera holds steady at a low angle. Atmosphere: focused precision. No lip-sync talker.
scenes.staging_notes: The hands-on-keyboard with visible editing action is the key beat. Aim for 3-4 seconds of clear typing-and-editing feel. End on a save button becoming active.

scenes.title: The Proof Moment — Right Panel Updates
scenes.narration: Here is the proof moment. Hit save, and the right panel updates instantly. The storyboard reflects the change, live.
scenes.visual_prompt: Cinematic medium shot of a wide monitor displaying a split-panel software interface in a dark workspace. The left edit panel shows a save action completing. The right storyboard preview panel visibly refreshes — the updated scene card text changes in real time with a brief highlight flash on the changed field. Camera slowly pushes in on the right panel as the update occurs. Soft warm rim light. The update flash is the hero beat — deliberate and unmistakable. No lip-sync talker.
scenes.staging_notes: This is the single clearest proof moment in the video. The right-panel update flash must read instantly. Slow the camera push-in to linger on the change. Overlay text fades in just after the update flash lands.

scenes.title: Callout: Why It Matters
scenes.narration: That live update is the key thing. No export, no reload. The edit shows up exactly where you need it, right away.
scenes.visual_prompt: Dark navy background with a single large centered statement in clean white sans-serif. Below it, two short contrast labels side by side: before on the left in muted red, after on the right in clean green. Minimal graphic dividers, soft glow behind the center text. Premium editorial motion feel.
scenes.staging_notes: Center statement animates in first with a confident scale-up. Then the two contrast labels slide in from their respective sides. Hold the full frame for 2-3 seconds. Pacing should feel like a punctuation mark in the story.

scenes.title: Preview Mode
scenes.narration: Once the storyboard looks right, preview mode plays it back as a complete sequence. Scenes in order, narration attached.
scenes.visual_prompt: Dark charcoal editorial interface illustration showing a full-width preview player occupying the top two-thirds of the screen. A timeline scrubber spans the bottom. Scene thumbnails are displayed in a horizontal strip below the player, with the active scene highlighted. A play button centered in the player. Clean minimal UI chrome, subtle inner glow on the player frame, premium SaaS aesthetic. No readable text in thumbnails beyond structural shape.
scenes.staging_notes: Two callout overlays: one pointing to the player labeled Preview mode, one pointing to the thumbnail strip labeled Scenes in order.

scenes.title: The Full Loop, Recapped
scenes.narration: So the loop is short. Paste, generate, edit if you need to, preview. Most runs need only one pass.
scenes.visual_prompt: Dark navy background, centered compact loop diagram. Four nodes arranged in a gentle arc or soft oval, connected by flowing arrows suggesting a loop. Nodes labeled with the four steps. One node — Edit — is visually smaller and slightly offset to signal it is optional. Clean iconography, premium motion editorial.
scenes.staging_notes: Nodes appear in sequence following the loop arc. The Edit node should visually feel secondary — smaller, labeled with a soft parenthetical. After all nodes appear, a gentle loop pulse animates around the full arc once.

scenes.title: Closing: Built for Operators
scenes.narration: This tool is built for operators who need to move fast without trading quality for speed. The workflow is short by design, and the proof is right there in the panel.
scenes.visual_prompt: Wide editorial image of a clean, minimal dark-mode software interface on a large monitor in a calm workspace. The storyboard panel is fully populated with neat scene cards. The room is dim with a warm desk lamp casting a focused pool of light on a keyboard in the foreground. The screen is crisp and bright in contrast. Composition: monitor centered, keyboard and lamp in shallow focus foreground, subtle depth suggesting a real working environment. Mood: resolved, confident, premium. No readable text in scene cards — only structural layout.
scenes.staging_notes: Overlay text fades in centered at the bottom of the frame against a subtle dark gradient bar. Let it sit cleanly for a full beat before the scene ends.
